tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate_A is asking me to take their part because they have a conflict, relying on my competence. I feel pressured to help immediately to keep the project on track, even though it wasn't in my original plan.

desires: Prove to teammate_A that I'm the only one who can keep this project from failing, even though it means overworking tonight.
Finish both tasks by tomorrow morning so I don't have to face anyone's disappointment about the deadline slipping again.
Avoid asking for an extension or explaining why I'm tired; just get it done and stay invisible until the work is submitted.

goals.description: Immediately execute teammate_A's assigned portion of the project to secure their gratitude and prove my indispensability, suppressing any fatigue or desire to refuse.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' axis demands I excel where others fail, and my 'avoidant' conflict style makes me terrified of saying no or creating friction. Refusing would risk their trust and force an awkward confrontation about capacity. Proving I'm the only one who can save this is a high-value academic outcome that outweighs my physical limits.

goals.description: Submit both tasks by tomorrow morning (Tue 09:00) without asking for an extension or explaining my exhaustion, maintaining the facade of effortless competence.
goals.rationale: While finishing early prevents future disappointment (a key driver), my 'impulsive' time horizon means I don't currently feel the weight of sleep debt or long-term burnout. The immediate need is to clear the backlog; worrying about the cost of overworking tonight feels like unnecessary friction until the work is actually done.

agent.last_reasoning: I'll crush this now to prove my indispensability and avoid the friction of saying no. It secures their gratitude immediately, but I'm ignoring my sleep debt and stress. Just power through tonight; worrying about burnout feels like unnecessary conflict right now.
